cases.name: All snippet analyses
cases.expectedStdout:
=== basic ===
order: A B micro timer
  A:sync
  B:sync
  micro:microtask
  timer:task
explanation: Sync logs run on the call stack first. Promise reactions are microtasks and drain before the setTimeout task runs.

=== async-await ===
order: A C B
  A:sync
  C:sync
  B:microtask
explanation: main() runs until await, which schedules the rest as a microtask. C prints before B resumes.

=== chained-microtasks ===
order: m1 m2 t1
  m1:microtask
  m2:microtask
  t1:task
explanation: Microtasks m1 and m2 drain completely before task t1 from setTimeout.

=== trick ===
order: start end micro timer
  start:sync
  end:sync
  micro:microtask
  timer:task
explanation: Microtasks always run before the next task, so micro prints before timer even with setTimeout(0).
